slides.title: Alphabet & Sounds
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 1 English, Unit 0: Alphabet & Sounds. Who This Is For ESL students aged 5–7 years. Absolute beginners with limited or no knowledge of the English alphabet. Children preparing to enter Cambridge Global English Year 1. What Children Will Be Able to Do In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Learner can recognise and name uppercase and lowercase letters
Learner can connect letters a-z with their most common sounds
Learner can hear and say beginning sounds in familiar words
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to learner can recognise and name uppercase and lowercase letters. you will also learner can connect letters a-z with their most common sounds. you will also learner can hear and say beginning sounds in familiar words. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: apple, ball, cat, dog, elephant, fish. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Week 1: Learn the Alphabet (A–Z) and Week 2: Phonics (Letter Sounds a–m). Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Language pattern 1
bullets: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: A says /a/.
slides.narration: The first language focus is Language pattern 1. Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: A says /a/. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Sound and say 1. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
